description_long: This object is a large, rectangular stone block with a flat base and twelve evenly spaced, upright rectangular prongs or pillars arranged in two parallel rows. The block appears to be made of solid, weathered concrete or stone, with a slightly mottled gray surface and subtle signs of wear. Each prong is hexagonal or polygonal in cross-section, with beveled edges and a uniform height, giving the block a robust, industrial appearance. The base is thick and solid, providing stability and weight, while the prongs are likely designed to prevent vehicles or large objects from passing over or through the block. The surface finish is matte and rough, with no visible engravings or markings. This type of barrier is commonly used in urban environments to control traffic flow or restrict access to certain areas.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with twelve upright prongs in two rows, made of rough, weathered gray concrete. It serves as a robust urban barrier to prevent vehicle passage.
description_view_0: Front view showing all twelve prongs in two parallel rows on a rectangular base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the block and the alignment of the prongs in two straight lines.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying the prongs and the solid base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the depth and spacing between the prongs.
attribute_location_context: urban street or parking lot barrier
attribute_installation_method: set on pavement or embedded in concrete
attribute_weathering_pattern: Slight surface discoloration and mottling, with minor wear on edges indicating exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
